output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Your Recovery Playbook
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to write a dispute letter that works.
content_outline: The Silent Treatment: Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit
Know the Deadline: How Long Your Landlord Legally Has to Return Your Deposit
Valid Charges vs. Bogus Fees: What Can a Landlord Deduct From Your Deposit
The 3-Email Demand Sequence to Recover Your Security Deposit Fast
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Escalation Guide: What to Do When Your Landlord Still Won't Give Your Deposit Back
key_points: State laws typically require deposit returns within 14 to 60 days; missing the deadline can trigger statutory penalties or double damages
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; only unpaid rent, excessive property damage, and lease-violation-related costs are legally valid
Email 1 — Subject: 'Re: Security deposit for [Address] — Return overdue' | Body: Formal request citing state deadline and forwarding address
Email 2 — Subject: 'Second Notice: Unreturned security deposit' | Body: Demand for itemized deductions and warning of legal consequences
Email 3 — Subject: 'Final Demand: Legal action pending' | Body: Ultimatum with specific deadline before small claims court filing
A professionally written security deposit dispute letter creates a legal paper trail and often forces immediate payment without court
output.cta: Stop chasing your landlord. Use DepositBack to generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in minutes and recover what's rightfully yours.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, not returning, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit and need urgent, actionable help
